message:
fix(vfs,pager): add cross-process file locking for WAL write safety

Previously, UnixFile::lock()/unlock() only tracked lock levels in memory
without issuing POSIX fcntl locks, and the pager never called file-level
locking at all. This meant multiple processes could write to the same
WAL file simultaneously, causing database corruption.

Changes:
- UnixFile::lock(): implement SQLite's five-level fcntl locking protocol
  (SHARED -> RESERVED -> PENDING -> EXCLUSIVE) with proper per-inode
  lock coalescing via InodeInfo counters
- UnixFile::unlock(): release OS-level locks only when the last handle
  drops its claim, matching POSIX fcntl semantics
- InodeInfo: add n_shared/n_reserved/n_pending/n_exclusive counters for
  cross-handle lock coalescing within the same process
- SimplePager::begin(): acquire SHARED lock for readers, RESERVED for
  eager writers (IMMEDIATE/EXCLUSIVE mode)
- SimplePager::ensure_writer(): acquire RESERVED when deferred
  transactions upgrade to writer
- commit_wal()/commit_journal(): escalate to EXCLUSIVE before writing
- commit()/rollback(): release file locks on transaction end
- Lock contention returns FrankenError::Busy for caller retry

Closes beads_rust#155.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
